title: Google Launches Gemini-Powered AI Agents for Advertising and Analytics
text:
SUBSCRIPTION ONLY
Artificial Intelligence
Google Launches Gemini-Powered AI Agents for Advertising and Analytics
The rollout follows the integration of an AI agent within Chrome earlier this year to help automate marketing tasks
A new bot integrated within Google Ads can build entire campaigns autonomously.
Rubaitul Azad, ADWEEK
By
Kendra Barnett
The future of brands gets decided here. Join the industry’s top marketers at Brandweek for the ideas, insights, and connections shaping what’s next.
Get your ticket.
Google on Wednesday debuted two new autonomous AI agents designed to help marketers optimize their advertising in real time and more easily track campaign performance.
Powered by Google’s flagship family of large language models, Gemini, the products act as “partners that learn from an advertiser’s unique datasets,” Dan Taylor, Google’s vice president of global ads, said on a call with press.
One of the products, Ads Advisor, is embedded within the Google Ads console. It uses AI to analyze a marketer’s unique data, specific business goals, landing pages, and campaign performance, and spits out personalized recommendations. A marketer might ask the agent, for instance, “How can I optimize my campaign for back-to-school season?” and the agent will offer specific tips, like adding sitelink extensions.
Kendra Barnett
Kendra Barnett is Adweek's senior tech reporter.
